Keywords: Use Dart's sound null safety to prevent null reference errors at compile time
Description: Use non-nullable types by default; use ? only when null is meaningful; use required for required params
Do: Use late everywhere; force-unwrap with !; ignore null warnings

Keywords: Add functionality to existing types without modifying them using extension methods
Description: Use extensions for type-specific utilities; keep extensions focused; name descriptively
Do: Create God extensions with 50+ methods; use extensions for complex business logic
Code Good: // Utility functions in a global file\nString capitalizeString(String s) => '${s[0].toUpperCase()}${s.substring(1)}';\nbool isValidEmail(String s) => ...; // Not discoverable

Keywords: Use const constructors for immutable widgets and objects to improve performance
Description: Add const to immutable widget constructors; use const for frequently reused widgets
Do: Ignore const warnings; create new instances of identical widgets every build

Keywords: Use named parameters for clarity especially with multiple parameters
Description: Use named params when function has 2+ params; mark essential params as required
Do: Use positional params for everything; pass booleans without naming

Keywords: Use Dart 3 patterns and sealed classes for exhaustive type-safe handling
Description: Use sealed classes for state; use switch expressions; destructure records
Do: Use if/else chains for type checking; ignore exhaustiveness warnings

Keywords: Use async/await properly with error handling and parallel execution
Description: Use Future.wait for parallel; handle errors; use stream for sequences
Do: Await in sequence when calls are independent; ignore async errors

Keywords: Prefer composing widgets from smaller widgets rather than inheritance
Description: Extract reusable widgets; compose using parameters; use callbacks for customization
Do: Extend StatelessWidget/StatefulWidget to create base classes; deep inheritance hierarchies
Code Good: // Inheritance anti-pattern\nclass BaseScreen extends StatefulWidget { ... }\nclass HomeScreen extends BaseScreen { ... } // Hard to customize, breaks composition

Keywords: Use Dart 3 records for returning multiple values and destructuring
Description: Use records for multi-value returns; destructure in assignments; name fields for clarity
Do: Return List or Map for multiple values; use out parameters

Keywords: Use keys correctly for widget identity and efficient list rendering
Description: Use ValueKey with unique identifiers; use UniqueKey when needed; use Key on list items
Do: Use GlobalKey everywhere; omit keys on dynamic list items; use index as key for reorderable lists

Keywords: Properly manage widget and app lifecycle events
Description: Implement dispose for controllers; use WidgetsBindingObserver for app lifecycle; cancel subscriptions
Do: Forget to dispose; ignore app background/foreground events; leak listeners

Keywords: Define descriptive type aliases for complex function signatures and generic types
Description: Use typedef for callback definitions; for complex generic types; for readability
Do: Create typedef for simple types that are already clear

Keywords: Use mixins to share behavior across unrelated classes
Description: Use mixins for cross-cutting behavior (logging analytics validation); keep mixins focused
Do: Use mixins as a replacement for composition; create God mixins with too many methods
